data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 finding.

Repo corroboration: the four enrichment methods 59ef puts under contract all resolve on the real AcliClient via MRO — AcliClient(AcliRestMixin, AcliGraphMixin) (acli.py L221); search_issues on the Rest side (acli.py L370); get_parent_map (acli_graph.py L189), get_comment_map (L289), get_issuelinks_map (L391) on the Graph mixin. The dual-run premise (one client surface, contract both real + fake) is sound; no sibling moves/renames these methods.

7-mode walk:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 59ef depends_on fe57 and exercises fe57's FakeAcliClient (_fakes.py) plus the real AcliClient; fe57 BUILDS the fake, 59ef VERIFIES it. 59ef adds tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_acliclient_contract.py and only reads acli_graph.py — no shared-file co-edit.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake serves fixtures through production enrichment with NO shape transformation") and 59ef AC2 ("fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") are mutually reinforcing — an unmassaged, fixture-faithful fake is exactly what passes 59ef's shape contract.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 59ef correctly declares depends_on fe57 (needs the fake for the hermetic leg); snapshot round-trip (→8937) and fixture capture (→fe57) are correctly OUT; it needs neither 8937's schema nor 822a's probe to stand up its harness.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 59ef contracts the enrichment METHODS' return shape (client surface); 8937 contracts the assembled SNAPSHOT-ENTRY schema; 822a asserts inbound↔outbound ROUND-TRIP. Three complementary angles, not duplicative.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. Only ordering constraint (fake before verifier) is captured by the declared depends_on fe57. No unsequenced same-region co-edit (the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier cluster touches neither 59ef's files nor its surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 59ef AC4 makes the live leg opt-in and guards against flaking/slowing the default suite (consistent with parent AC7 "no net increase in brittle tests"); AC3 asserts structure/type not exact values, imposing no brittle expectation on consumers.
- Residual references — BENIGN. All four methods exist on the real client; 33d0 collapses the OUTBOUND applier (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), not the INBOUND enrichment surface 59ef contracts, so its deletion strands nothing here.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 coverage matter already tracked across the sibling set and this ticket's prior audits — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not re-filed here.

No G4 cross-child finding for this pairing; all 7 modes read benign.
